summary: Large mncharsel character-select decomp/matching PR centered on adding a typed C implementation of mnCharSel_802640A0, tightening several nearby CSS functions, and introducing header/type fixes for CSS scene assets and SisLib text state. The bot report shows main/melee/mn/mncharsel mnCharSel_802640A0 improved from 0.00% to 86.22%, with smaller mncharsel gains in fn_802633B0, mnCharSel_8025FDEC, mnCharSel_8025FB50, fn_8025F0E0, fn_80262648, mnCharSel_8025D1C4, mnCharSel_CursorThink, and mnCharSel_8025DB34.
key_files: src/melee/mn/mncharsel.c: +974/-74; added mnCharSel_802640A0 and many match-tuning edits in existing CSS functions.
src/melee/mn/mncharsel.h: changed mnCharSel_802640A0 prototype from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to s32 mnCharSel_802640A0(void).
src/melee/mn/mncharsel.static.h: added CSSSceneModels, CSSAnimSet, static HSD_GObj globals mnCharSel_804D6CB8/mnCharSel_804D6CBC, and changed an xd3 initializer to NULL.
src/melee/mn/types.h: changed CSSDoorsData.xd3 from float to HSD_Text*.

target_funcs.txt: added checked target list for mncharsel functions including mnCharSel_802640A0, fn_802633B0, fn_80262648, fn_8025F0E0, mnCharSel_CursorThink, mnCharSel_8025FB50, mnCharSel_8025FDEC, mnCharSel_8025D1C4, mnCharSel_8025DB34, mnCharSel_8025C020, and mnCharSel_80266D70_OnLeave.
what_changed: Implemented mnCharSel_802640A0 as a large character-select setup routine that creates camera, light, fog, background, cursor, character model, tag/name-list, stock/score text, and mode-specific UI objects using HSD GObj/JObj/SisLib APIs.

Added typed scene asset descriptors CSSSceneModels and CSSAnimSet, then used local MODELS and ANIM macros to cast existing void-pointer statics mnCharSel_804D6CB4 and mnCharSel_804D6CD8 for the new setup function.
Added MenMain_cam assignment and static GXColor constants mnCharSel_804DC580 through mnCharSel_804DC594 for the newly decompiled text/color setup.
Expanded baselib includes in mncharsel.c for forward declarations, camera, gobj, gobjobject, gobjproc, gobjuserdata, lobj, memory, and related HSD object creation functions.
Retyped CSSDoorsData.xd3 as HSD_Text*, which removed casts around HSD_SisLib_803A746C and made the one-player difficulty scroll text state typed instead of a float placeholder.

Adjusted matching-sensitive local variable widths and casts in several existing functions, especially handicap values changed from u8 locals to s32 with explicit u8 casts, c_kind/icon comparisons using s8/int, and grabbed/player locals using int.
Simplified or reshaped codegen in fn_8025F0E0 and fn_802633B0 by replacing some pointer aliases and cached sel_icon variables with direct mnCharSel_803F0DFC field access.

Changed mnCharSel_8025FB50 costume selection into an explicit while loop that writes costume before checking isDuplicateCostume, and kept a CSSIcon table pointer computed as (CSSIcon*)(&mnCharSel_803F0A48 + 1).

Adjusted object linking and model positioning details, including HSD_GObjGXLink_803909D8 using mnCharSel_804A0BC0[mnCharSel_804D6CF5 - 1] in mnCharSel_8025FDEC and fn_80262648 using cursor data arrays for some offsets.

Decomp-dev bot reported overall matched code 69.17% (+0.04%, +1600 bytes) and matched data 41.19% (+32 bytes), but also reported one broken mncharsel match: mnCharSel_8026688C_OnEnter from 100.00% to 99.90%.
smart_moves: Before decompiling the giant initializer, the PR introduced small typed asset structs for camera/light/fog and joint/anim/matanim/shapeanim bundles, making the large function readable without inventing semantic field names beyond evidence.

The new mnCharSel_802640A0 mirrors HSD setup order carefully: GObj_Create, object load/attach, GXLink setup, proc setup, AddAnimAll, ReqAnimAll/AnimAll, and ForeachAnim stop calls appear in consistent scene-graph sequences.
The code uses scoped MODELS and ANIM macros and undefines them after the function, keeping raw global casts localized.
Matching-sensitive casts are explicit rather than hidden in type changes: gm_801685D4 results are cast to u8 even when stored in s32, and c_kind comparisons retain signed s8 behavior where needed.
The HSD_Text* retype for CSSDoorsData.xd3 is a high-leverage header fix because it cleans up scroll text callsites and makes the static initializer semantically NULL.
The decomp keeps low-level constants, Shift-JIS byte strings, joint IDs, AOBJ args, JOBJ flags, and enum-like match_type cases visible rather than prematurely over-abstracting uncertain semantics.
The switch in mnCharSel_802640A0 preserves fallthrough/goto structure via a hide_extra label for shared mode-specific UI hiding, a practical tactic for matching large control flow.
decomp_lessons: For large menu initialization functions, define the resource descriptor structs first; typed asset bundles make object creation code tractable and can improve matching without requiring full semantic renaming.
Header/type fixes can be as important as body edits: changing a placeholder float to HSD_Text* converted awkward casts into normal SisLib calls and clarified the CSSDoorsData layout.
Do not assume smaller integer locals always match source intent; this PR repeatedly used s32 locals plus explicit u8/s8 casts to shape sign/zero extension and branch codegen.
Pointer aliases are not always match-friendly. Some earlier aliases for scroll_flag/page fields and sel_icon were removed in favor of direct global field access to better match generated code.

A target_funcs checklist is useful for tracking work, but the bot evidence shows mnCharSel_802640A0 was still only 86.22% after this PR, so checked targets should not be treated as proof of perfect matching.

Automated decomp reports can include cross-unit matches/regressions outside the changed-file slice; for postmortems, attribute the PR mainly to files actually changed unless the diff supports broader claims.
naming_conventions: Unsemantized functions retain project address-style names such as mnCharSel_802640A0, fn_8025F0E0, fn_80262648, and fn_802633B0.
Character Select Screen data types use the CSS prefix: CSSSceneModels, CSSAnimSet, CSSIcon, CSSData, CSSDoor, CSSDoorsData, CSSTagData.
Static/global symbols keep DOL-address suffixes, for example mnCharSel_804D6CB8, mnCharSel_804D6CBC, and mnCharSel_804DC580.
Locals use short but evidence-based names such as match_type, num_players, row_a, row_b, icons, player, hval, door, model, cursor, text, and ctx.
Temporary typed macros MODELS and ANIM are used only around the new implementation and then undefined.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Computed the CSS icon table with type punning: CSSIcon* icons = (CSSIcon*)(&mnCharSel_803F0A48 + 1).
Used explicit u8 and s8 casts around gm_801685D4 values, c_kind, sel_icon, match_type, and player fields to control extension and comparisons.
Retained PAD_STACK(8) in mnCharSel_8025FB50 after changing local layout, indicating stack padding was still needed for codegen.
Used direct global field access for mnCharSel_803F0DFC.xcd/xce/xcf/scroll_flag instead of local u8* aliases in the scrolling branch.
Matched animation calls with exact HSD_ForeachAnim masks and args such as JOBJ_TYPE, ALL_TYPE_MASK, TOBJ_MASK, JOBJ_MASK, AOBJ_ARG_AF, and AOBJ_ARG_AOV.
Copied static GXColor values into stack locals before HSD_SisLib_803A74F0 calls, preserving likely stack/color-passing behavior.
Preserved control-flow quirks like an empty-if/else assignment for hval defaults in some places and a hide_extra goto/fallthrough path in the mode switch.
Used NULL text initialization in HSD_SisLib_803A6B98 calls and raw Shift-JIS byte strings for menu labels rather than replacing them with speculative English names.
review_feedback: No human PR body, review comments, or reviews were present in the slice.
The only comment was the decomp-dev bot report for GALE01.
Bot report: matched code 69.17% (+0.04%, +1600 bytes) and matched data 41.19% (+32 bytes).
Bot report: main/melee/mn/mncharsel mnCharSel_802640A0 improved by +8811 bytes from 0.00% to 86.22%.
Bot report: one broken mncharsel match remained, mnCharSel_8026688C_OnEnter from 100.00% to 99.90%.
Bot report also listed gr/grpushon, gr/grpura, and gr/grgreens changes, but those files are not in the changed_files slice, so their relationship to this PR is weak from the available evidence.
follow_up_queries: CSSDoorsData xd3 HSD_Text scroll_flag xcd xce xcf
mncharsel handicap gm_801685D4 s32 u8 cast
mncharsel target_funcs jj/mncharsel3 mnCharSel_8026688C_OnEnter broken match
